puzzle0.title: ARCHIVE DECRYPTION
puzzle0.description: An encrypted archive fragment has been detected. The correct key is required to access the content.

puzzle1.title: INCIDENT CORRELATION
puzzle1.description: Match the recovered records with the correct conclusions.

event1.text: The report you just saw wasn't a standard error log.

event2.text: Door 12 was opened. Followed by a medical trauma report, a manual override, and a scrubbed security trace.

event3.text: These records aren't corrupted. They're incomplete.

event4.text: It looks like someone specifically removed certain pieces.

event0.text: I wish I had the answer ready.

event1.text: But right now, what I'm hiding from you isn't a fact I know. It's a gap I can't remember.

event2.text: If I find out I've been burying this on purpose... you'll hear it from me first.

event1.text: Panic can stay here for later. Unfortunately, panic is a very loyal thing.

event1.title: ARCHIVE MAP
event1.content:
Recovered maintenance map of the station archive sector.

Sector A: Primary relay archive
Sector B: Corrupted personnel cache
Sector C: Emergency channel logs

event3.text: This shouldn't still be indexed. Either someone was very careless, or they wanted us to find this.

event1.text: Brave. I usually call that 'confidence I'll regret later'.
Checking the pulse before poking a sleeping system with your hand—surprisingly healthy approach.

event0.text: There might still be something salvageable inside.

event1.text: If the archive survived, the answers might have survived, too.

event3.text: The worse possibility is that someone specifically wanted these answers to survive.

event4.title: ENCRYPTED STORAGE DEVICE
event4.content:
[ENCRYPTED ARCHIVE FRAGMENT]

Archive Key Hint: ALPHA

Warning: Failed decryption attempts may trigger a quarantine response.

Status: Locked

event1.text: Stop. The archive noticed the wrong key.

event2.text: Try the hint exactly. Don't guess.

event3.text: I'm out of patience for systems that punish curiosity today.

event1.title: LOG TRANSCRIPT
event1.content:
21:14 Door 12 opened.
21:15 Unidentified movement detected.
21:17 Security override accepted.
21:19 Signal interruption.

event3.text: This timeline feels familiar. It shouldn't feel familiar.

event1.text: At least... I thought I deleted it.

event2.text: You delete a file, it disappears. That's how it's supposed to work. ECHO seems to view 'behaving normally' as a personal insult.

event0.text: Some records were never meant to survive.

event1.text: And now I'm starting to think this didn't survive by accident.

event2.text: Someone left these pieces out just enough for us to find, but too incomplete for us to understand.

event1.title: CORRIDOR CAMERA ANALYSIS
event1.content:
SOURCE: Door 12 archive feed
Visual frame could not be recovered.
Automatic analysis result:
- One robed figure detected.
- Suit outlines match ECHO engineering production.
- Object in right hand: manual override lever.
- Timestamp damaged.
- Identity certainty: 64%
event1.caption: Automatic analysis report generated from Door 12 archive feed. Visual frame could not be recovered.

event3.text: That's high enough. And I don't like it.

event4.text: The angle is bad, the log is corrupted... but the suit outlines are familiar.

event5.text: I hate that the evidence has a better memory than I do.

event1.title: SECURITY OVERRIDE LOG
event1.content:
Override User: REDACTED
Authorization: VALID
Source: UNKNOWN
event1.message: [SECURITY OVERRIDE LOG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event3.text: Security logs don't work like that. If a door opens, the system knows who opened it.

event4.text: Either the log is corrupted... or someone didn't want the system to know.

event1.text: Separately, the pieces are meaningless. But looking at them together, they point in the same direction.

event2.text: The door log, camera analysis, and override trace are different pieces of the same event. Try again.

event1.title: EMERGENCY CHANNEL NOTE
event1.content:
Emergency Backup Channel

7.14 MHz

Use only if primary communication systems fail.

Warning: Do not open if an unauthorized return signal is detected.
event1.message: [EMERGENCY CHANNEL NOTE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event3.text: No. We don't touch that channel.

event4.text: I'm not saying this because I'm afraid.

event5.text: Okay, I'm saying it because I'm a little afraid. But I have good reasons.

event1.text: Whatever happens from now on, don't open the 7.14 channel.

event2.text: If it promises you help, don't open it. If it says it will answer, don't open it.

event3.text: Some doors are safer when they stay locked.

event1.text: We thought backup channels were safer.

event2.text: But something in ECHO learned how to masquerade as a security protocol.

event4.text: If you can hear me, close it. Now.
